prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: The yeoman of the realm hath returned with tidings of the third quarter's bounty, yet my mind doth reel at the fantastical figures he bringeth. It is said that the firm hath increased its customer base by a score and seven percent, a feat akin to a peasant tilling a field of gold. The scribes of the company whisper of a revenue growth of thrice and ten percent, a sum that would feed our kingdom for a year and a day. Alas, I fear I have fallen into a dream, for how can a business thrive without the till of the land or the toil of the forge?
